originalData.title: N-methylmesoporphyrin
originalData.definition: A mesoporphyrin with an N-methyl substituent at the 22-position.

originalData.title: coenzyme F430
originalData.definition: A nickel tetrapyrrole found only in methanogenic Archaea. It is the prosthetic group of the enzyme methyl coenzyme M reductase which catalyses the release of methane in the final step of methanogenesis.

originalData.title: precorrin-4
originalData.definition: The intermediate in the biosynthesis of vitamin B<small><sub>12</sub></small> from uroporphyrinogen III in which four methyl groups have been introduced at positions 2, 7, 17 and 20 of the tetrapyrrole framework, together with ring contraction of the framework forming a bond between C-1 and C-19, leaving C-20 as part of an acetyl group bonded to C-1.

originalData.title: precorrin-6X
originalData.definition: The intermediate in the biosynthesis of vitamin B<small><sub>12</sub></small> from uroporphyrinogen III in which six methyl groups have been introduced into the tetrapyrrole framework, together with ring contraction.

originalData.title: precorrin-5
originalData.definition: The intermediate in the biosynthesis of vitamin B<small><sub>12</sub></small> from uroporphyrinogen III in which five methyl groups have been introduced into the tetrapyrrole framework, together with ring contraction.

originalData.title: precorrin-3B
originalData.definition: The intermediate in the biosynthesis of vitamin B<small><sub>12</sub></small> from uroporphyrinogen III in which three methyl groups have been introduced at positions 2, 7 and 20 of the tetrapyrrole framework and in which the carboxymethyl group at position 2 has been oxidised to form a lactone ring.

originalData.title: precorrin-6Y
originalData.definition: The intermediate in the biosynthesis of vitamin B<small><sub>12</sub></small> from uroporphyrinogen III in which six methyl groups have been introduced into the tetrapyrrole framework, together with ring contraction and reduction.

originalData.title: precorrin-3A
originalData.definition: The intermediate in the biosynthesis of vitamin B<small><sub>12</sub></small> from uroporphyrinogen III in which three methyl groups have been introduced at positions 2, 7 and 20 of the tetrapyrrole framework.

originalData.title: precorrin-8X
originalData.definition: The intermediate in the biosynthesis of vitamin B<small><sub>12</sub></small> from uroporphyrinogen III in which eight methyl groups have been introduced into the tetrapyrrole framework, together with ring contraction and decarboxylation.

originalData.title: precorrin-1
originalData.definition: The first intermediate in the biosynthesis of vitamin B<small><sub>12</sub></small> from uroporphyrinogen III, in which one methyl group has been introduced at position 2 of the tetrapyrrole framework.

originalData.title: precorrin-7
originalData.definition: A precorrin containing seven C-methyl groups introduced into the tetrapyrrole framework.

originalData.title: emamectin
originalData.definition: A mixture of emamectin B<small><sub>1a</sub></small> (major component) and emamectin B<small><sub>1b</sub></small> (minor component).

originalData.title: milbemectin
originalData.definition: Mixture of 70% of milbemycin A<small><sub>4</sub></small> and 30% of milbemycin A<small><sub>3</sub></small>.

originalData.title: 1-acylglycerone 3-phosphate
originalData.definition: A glycerone 3-phosphate having an unspecified O-acyl substituent at the 1-position.

originalData.title: cyclohexane-1,4-dione
originalData.definition: A cyclohexanedione with oxo substituents at positions 1 and 4.

originalData.title: cyclohexane-1,2-dione
originalData.definition: A cyclohexanedione carrying oxo substituents at positions 1 and 2.

originalData.title: cyclohex-2-enone
originalData.definition: A cyclohexenone having its C=C double bond at the 2-position.

originalData.title: 1,2-benzoquinone
originalData.definition: A benzoquinone resulting from the formal oxidation of catechol.

originalData.title: thymoquinone
originalData.definition: A member of the class of 1,4-benzoquinones that is 1,4-bezoquinone in which the hydrogens at positions 2 and 5 are replaced by methyl and isopropyl groups, respectively. It is a natural compound isolated from Nigella sativa which has demonstrated promising chemotherapeutic activity.

originalData.title: ubiquinones
originalData.definition: Any benzoquinone derived from 2,3-dimethoxy-5-methylbenzoquinone; one of a group of naturally occurring homologues. The redox-active quinoid moiety usually carries a polyprenoid side chain at position 6, the number of isoprenoid units in which is species-specific. Ubiquinones are involved in the control of mitochondrial electron transport, and are also potent anti-oxidants.

originalData.title: 1,4-benzoquinone
originalData.definition: The simplest member of the class of 1,4-benzoquinones, obtained by the formal oxidation of hydroquinone to the corresponding diketone. It is a metabolite of benzene.

originalData.title: rhodoquinones
originalData.definition: Rhodoquinone (RQ) is a modified ubiquinone, where the methoxyl group in position 3 is replaced by an amino group.

originalData.title: polyprenylbenzoquinone
originalData.definition: Any member of the class of 1,4-benzoquinones substituted by a polyprenyl-derived side-chain.

originalData.title: 2-prenyl-1,4-benzoquinone
originalData.definition: A member of the class of 1,4-benzoquinones that is 1,4-benzoquinone in which the hydrogen at position 2 has been replaced by a prenyl group.

originalData.title: duroquinone
originalData.definition: A member of the class of 1,4-benzoquinones that is 1,4-benzoquinone in which all four hydrogens are substituted by methyl groups.

originalData.title: (2Z,10Z,26Z)-ubiquinone
originalData.definition: A geometric isomer of ubiquinone-10 in which the double bonds at positions 2, 10 and 26 are in the Z conformation.

originalData.title: 6-decylubiquinone
originalData.definition: A member of the class of 1,4-benzoquinones that is 2,3-dimethoxybenzoquinone which has been substituted at positions 5 and 6 by decyl and methyl groups.

originalData.title: decylplastoquinone
originalData.definition: A member of the class of 1,4-benzoquinones in which the quinone ring is substituted at positions 2 and 3 by methyl groups and at position 5 by a decyl group.

originalData.title: primin
originalData.definition: A 1,4-benzoquinone having a methoxy substituent at the 2-position and a pentyl substituent at the 6-position.

originalData.title: 1,2-naphthoquinone
originalData.definition: The parent structure of the family of 1,2-naphthoquinones, in which the oxo groups of the quinone moiety are at positions 1 and 2 of the naphthalene ring. It is a metabolite of naphthalene and is found in diesel exhaust particles.

originalData.title: 1,4-naphthoquinone
originalData.definition: The parent structure of the family of 1,4-naphthoquinones, in which the oxo groups of the quinone moiety are at positions 1 and 4 of the naphthalene ring. Derivatives have pharmacological properties.

originalData.title: vitamin K
originalData.definition: Any member of a group of fat-soluble 2-methyl-1,4-napthoquinones that exhibit biological activity against vitamin K deficiency. Vitamin K is required for the synthesis of prothrombin and certain other blood coagulation factors.

originalData.title: menadione
originalData.definition: A member of the class of 1,4-naphthoquinones that is 1,4-naphthoquinone which is substituted at position 2 by a methyl group. It is used as a nutritional supplement and for the treatment of hypoprothrombinemia.

originalData.title: 2,3-dimethoxynaphthalene-1,4-dione
originalData.definition: A naphthoquinone that is 1,4-naphthoquinone bearing two methoxy substituents at positions 2 and 3. Redox-cycling agent that induces intracellular superoxide anion formation and, depending on the concentration, induces cell proliferation, apoptosis or necrosis. Used to study the role of ROS in cell toxicity, apoptosis, and necrosis.

originalData.title: 3-demethyl-5-methylmenaquinone
originalData.definition: A member of the class of prenylnapthoquinones that carries a methyl group at position 5.

originalData.title: menaquinones
originalData.definition: Any prenylnaphthoquinone having a prenyl or polyprenyl group at position 3 and a methyl group at position 2 on the naphthoquinone ring.

originalData.title: L-dopachrome
originalData.definition: The <small>L</small>-enantiomer of dopachrome.

originalData.title: D-dopachrome
originalData.definition: The <small>D</small>-enantiomer of dopachrome.

originalData.title: 2-(3-methylbut-2-enyl)-5-(2-methylbut-3-en-2-yl)cyclohexa-2,5-diene-1,4-dione
originalData.definition: A natural product found in Sporochnus comosus and Perithalia capillaris.

originalData.title: (8Z)-heptadecenal
originalData.definition: An 8-heptadecenal in which the double bond at position 8 adopts a cis-configuration.

originalData.title: (8E)-heptadecenal
originalData.definition: An 8-heptadecenal in which the double bond at position 8 adopts a trans-configuration.

originalData.title: 13-methyltetradecenal
originalData.definition: A monounsaturated fatty aldehyde consisting of tetradecenal in which the position of the C=C double bond is not defined and in which a hydrogen at position 13 has been replaced by a methyl group.

originalData.title: hexenal
originalData.definition: A monounsaturated fatty acid aldehyde that is hexene having an oxo group at position 1.

originalData.title: (10Z,12E)-hexadecadienal
originalData.definition: A polyunsaturated fatty aldehyde that is hexadecadienal in which the two double bonds are located at positions 10 and 12 (the 10Z,12E-geoisomer).

originalData.title: (2E,4E)-deca-2,4-dienal
originalData.definition: A polyunsaturated fatty aldehyde that is decanal which has undergone formal dehydrogenation to introduce trans- double bonds at the 2-3 and 4-5 positions. A product of lipid peroxidation in cell membranes and a component of cooking oil fumes.

originalData.title: octadecadienal
originalData.definition: Any C<small><sub>18</sub></small> polyunsaturated fatty aldehyde containing two double bonds.

originalData.title: tetradecadienal
originalData.definition: Any C<small><sub>14</sub></small> polyunsaturated fatty aldehyde containing two double bonds.

originalData.title: (8Z,11Z,14Z)-heptadecatrienal
originalData.definition: A polyunsaturated fatty aldehyde that is heptadecenal having three double bonds located at postions 8, 11 and 14 (the 8Z,11Z,14Z-geoisomer).

originalData.title: (3R)-hydroxy-11Z-octadecenoyl-L-carnitine
originalData.definition: A 3-hydroxy-11Z-octadecenoyl-L-carnitine in which the chiral carbon attached to the hydroxy group adopts (R)-configuration

originalData.title: 1-(9Z-octadecenoyl)-2-(7Z,10Z,13Z-hexadecatrienoyl)-3-beta-D-galactosyl-sn-glycerol
originalData.definition: A monogalactosyldiacylglycerol 34:4 in which the 1- and 2-acyl groups are specified as oleoyl and (7Z,10Z,13Z)-hexadecatrienoyl respectively.

originalData.title: (25R)-3alpha,7alpha,12alpha-trihydroxy-5beta-cholestan-26-oyl-CoA
originalData.definition: A 3α,7α,12α-trihydroxy-5β-cholestan-26-oyl-CoA in which the carbon at position 25 of the steroidal side chain has R configuration.

originalData.title: (25S)-3alpha,7alpha,12alpha-trihydroxy-5beta-cholestanoyl-CoA
originalData.definition: A 3α,7α,12α-trihydroxy-5β-cholestan-26-oyl-CoA in which the carbon at position 25 of the steroidal side chain has S configuration.

originalData.title: (24R,25R)-3alpha,7alpha,12alpha,24-tetrahydroxy-5beta-cholestan-26-oyl-CoA
originalData.definition: An acyl-CoA that results from the formal condensation of the thiol group of coenzyme A with the carboxy group of (24R,25R)-3α,7α,12α,24-tetrahydroxy-5β-cholestan-26-oic acid.

originalData.title: (3S)-3-carboxy-3-hydroxypropanoyl-CoA
originalData.definition: A 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA that results from the formal condensation of the thiol group of coenzyme A with the 3-carboxy group of (3S)-3-carboxy-3-hydroxypropanoic acid.

originalData.title: (3S)-citryl-CoA
originalData.definition: An (S)-3-hydroxyacyl-CoA having (3S)-citryl as the S-acyl group

originalData.title: (S)-3-hydroxyoctanedioyl-CoA
originalData.definition: An (S)-3-hydroxyacyl-CoA resulting from the formal condensation of the thiol group of coenzyme A with the 1-carboxy group of (S)-3-hydroxyoctanedioic acid.

originalData.title: (3S)-hydroxytetradecanedioyl-CoA
originalData.definition: An (S)-3-hydroxyacyl-CoA resulting from the formal condensation of the thiol group of coenzyme A with the 1-carboxy group of (S)-3-hydroxytetradecanedioic acid.

originalData.title: (3S)-hydroxyhexadecanedioyl-CoA
originalData.definition: An (S)-3-hydroxyacyl-CoA resulting from the formal condensation of the thiol group of coenzyme A with the 1-carboxy group of (3S)-hydroxyhexadecanedioic acid.

originalData.title: (3R)-hydroxyhexadecanedioyl-CoA
originalData.definition: An (R)-3-hydroxyacyl-CoA resulting from the formal condensation of the thiol group of coenzyme A with the 1-carboxy group of (3R)-hydroxyhexadecanedioic acid.

originalData.title: (3R)-3-carboxy-3-hydroxypropanoyl-CoA
originalData.definition: A (3R)-3-hydroxyacyl-CoA that results from the formal condensation of the thiol group of coenzyme A with the 3-carboxy group of (3R)-3-carboxy-3-hydroxypropanoic acid.

originalData.title: (R)-3-hydroxybutanoyl-CoA
originalData.definition: The (R)-enantiomer of 3-hydroxybutanoyl-CoA.

originalData.title: Fe2S2 ferredoxin
originalData.definition: A ferredoxin compound having a [Fe2S2] cluster as the active centre, where the iron atoms are tetrahedrally coordinated both by inorganic sulfur atoms and by sulfurs provided by four conserved cysteine (Cys) residues.

originalData.title: ferricytochrome
originalData.definition: Any cytochrome containing a ferriheme.

originalData.title: ferrocytochrome
originalData.definition: Any cytochrome containing a ferroheme.

originalData.title: 1-O-glyceryl beta-D-galactopyranoside
originalData.definition: A 3-<small>D</small>-galactosyl glycerol in which the carbon bearing the anomeric hydrogen has β configuration.

originalData.title: N(6')-acetylkanamycin B
originalData.definition: An N6'-acetylkanamycin derived from kanamycin B.

originalData.title: N-acetyl-beta-D-galactosamine 4-sulfate
originalData.definition: A galactosamine sulfate that is N-acetyl-β-D-galactosamine in which the hydroxy group at position 4 has been converted into its sulfate derivative.

originalData.title: N-acetyl-beta-D-galactosamine 6-sulfate
originalData.definition: A galactosamine sulfate that is N-acetyl-β-D-galactosamine in which the hydroxy group at position 6 has been converted into its sulfate derivative.

originalData.title: alpha-D-fructuronate
originalData.definition: The α-anomer of <small>D</small>-fructuronate.

originalData.title: beta-D-fructuronate
originalData.definition: The β-anomer of <small>D</small>-fructuronate.

originalData.title: (25R)-3-oxocholest-4-en-26-oyl-CoA(4-)
originalData.definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) resulting from the removal of all four protons from the phosphate and diphosphate groups of (25R)-3-oxocholest-4-en-26-oyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

originalData.title: (2R)-ibuprofenoyl-CoA(4-)
originalData.definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) resulting from the removal of all four protons from the phosphate and diphosphate groups of (2R)-ibuprofenoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

originalData.title: (2S)-ibuprofenoyl-CoA(4-)
originalData.definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) resulting from the removal of all four protons from the phosphate and diphosphate groups of (2S)-ibuprofenoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

originalData.definition: A myo-inositol bisphosphate in which the two phosphate groups are located at positions 2 and 4.

originalData.title: 1D-myo-inositol 3,5-bisphosphate

originalData.title: myo-inositol hexakisphosphate

originalData.definition: A myo-inositol pentakisphosphate comprising 1D-myo-inositol having the five phosphate groups placed in the 1-' 2-' 4-, 5- and 6-positions.

originalData.title: 6-diphospho-1D-myo-inositol pentakisphosphate

originalData.title: 1D-myo-inositol 1,3,4,5-tetrakisphosphate
originalData.definition: A myo-inositol tetrakisphosphate having the four phosphates placed in the 1-, 3-, 4- and 5-positions.

originalData.title: 1-diphospho-1D-myo-inositol 3,4,5,6-tetrakisphosphate
originalData.definition: A myo-inositol tetrakisphosphate that consists of myo-inositol having the four phospho groups located at positions 3, 4, 5 and 6 as well as a diphospho group at position 1.

originalData.title: 3-diphospho-1D-myo-inositol 1,4,5,6-tetrakisphosphate

originalData.title: 1D-myo-inositol 3,4,6-trisphosphate
originalData.definition: A myo-inositol trisphosphate having the three phosphate groups at the 3-, 4-, and 6-positions.

originalData.title: CMP-3-deoxy-beta-D-manno-octulosonic acid
originalData.definition: A CMP-sugar having 3-deoxy-β-D-manno-octulosonic acid as the sugar component.

originalData.title: TDP-alpha-D-glucose

originalData.title: dTDP-4-dehydro-6-deoxy-D-galactose

originalData.title: TDP-beta-L-rhamnose

originalData.title: 1-(5-phospho-beta-D-ribosyl)-5'-AMP
originalData.definition: An AMP-sugar in which the hydrogen at position 1 of AMP is substituted by a 5-phospho-β-<small>D</small>-ribosyl group.

originalData.title: 2'-(5-phosphoribosyl)-5'-AMP
originalData.definition: An AMP-sugar in which the 2'-hydroxy group of AMP carries a 5-phospho-β-<small>D</small>-ribosyl substituent. Monomer unit of poly(ADP-Rib) [poly(adenosine diphosphate ribose)].

originalData.title: 2'-[2-(5-phosphoribosyl)-5-phosphoribosyl]-5'-AMP
originalData.definition: An AMP-sugar in which the 2'-hydroxy group of AMP carries a 5-phospho-β-<small>D</small>-ribosyl substituent, the 2-hydroxy group of which itself carries a further 5-phospho-β-<small>D</small>-ribosyl substituent. Branched portion of poly(ADP-Rib) [poly(adenosine diphosphate ribose)].

originalData.title: GDP-4-dehydro-6-deoxy-alpha-D-mannose
originalData.definition: A GDP-sugar having 4-dehydro-6-deoxy-α-<small>D</small>-mannose as the sugar portion.

originalData.title: GDP-D-mannuronic acid
originalData.definition: A GDP-sugar having <small>D</small>-mannuronic acid as the sugar component.

originalData.title: GDP-hexose
originalData.definition: A GDP-sugar in which the sugar component is a hexosyl residue.

originalData.title: GDP-4-dehydro-6-deoxy-L-mannose
originalData.definition: A GDP-sugar having 4-dehydro-6-deoxy-<small>L</small>-mannose as the sugar component.

originalData.title: GDP-6-deoxy-L-mannose
originalData.definition: A GDP-sugar having 6-deoxy-<small>L</small>-mannose as the sugar component.

originalData.title: GDP-3,6-dideoxy-D-galactose
originalData.definition: A GDP-sugar having 3,6-dideoxy-<small>D</small>-galactose as the sugar component.

originalData.title: GDP-D-glycero-alpha-D-manno-heptose
originalData.definition: A GDP-sugar in which the sugar component is D-glycero-α-D-manno-heptose.

originalData.title: GDP-4-dehydro-3,6-dideoxy-alpha-D-mannose
originalData.definition: A GDP-sugar having 4-dehydro-3,6-dideoxy-α-<small>D</small>-mannose as the sugar fragment.

originalData.title: GDP-beta-L-colitose
originalData.definition: A GDP-sugar having β-<small>L</small>-colitose as the sugar fragment.

originalData.title: ADP-D-ribose
originalData.definition: A nucleotide-sugar having ADP as the nucleotide fragment and <small>D</small>-ribofuranos-5-yl as the sugar component.

originalData.title: D-guloside
originalData.definition: Any guloside in which the glycoside group is derived from <small>D</small>-gulose.

originalData.title: L-guloside
originalData.definition: Any guloside in which the glycoside group is derived from <small>L</small>-gulose.

originalData.title: L-galactoside
originalData.definition: Any galactoside having <small>L</small>-configuration.

originalData.title: D-glucoside
originalData.definition: Any glucoside in which the glycoside group is derived from <small>D</small>-glucose.

originalData.title: alpha-glucoside
originalData.definition: A glucoside in which the anomeric carbon of the glycosidic bond is in an α configuration

originalData.title: beta-glucoside
originalData.definition: A glucoside in which the anomeric carbon of the glycosidic bond is in a β configuration

originalData.title: L-glucoside
originalData.definition: Any glucoside with an <small>L</small>-configuration.

originalData.title: alpha-D-mannoside
originalData.definition: Any mannoside in which the anomeric centre has α-configuration.

originalData.title: ditrans,polycis-dodecaprenyl phosphate-GalA
originalData.definition: A galactosiduronic acid resulting from the formal condensation of the anomeric hydroxy group of β-D-galactopyranuronic acid with the phosphate group of ditrans,polycis-dodecaprenyl phosphate.

originalData.title: 3-aminopropyl beta-D-galactopyranosiduronic acid
originalData.definition: A galactosiduronic acid in which β-<small>D</small>-galacturonic acid is linked glycosidically to a 3-aminopropyl group.

originalData.title: beta-L-fucoside
originalData.definition: A <small>L</small>-fucoside with a β-configuration.

originalData.title: rhamnofuranoside
originalData.definition: Any rhamnoside that exists in the furanose form.

originalData.title: alpha-D-rhamnoside
originalData.definition: Any rhamnoside that exists in the pyranose form and has α-<small>D</small>-configuration.

originalData.title: N-acetyl-beta-D-glucosaminyl-(1->4)-N-acetyl-beta-D-glucosamine
originalData.definition: An N,N'-diacetylchitobiose having β-configuration at the reducing end anomeric centre.

originalData.title: beta-D-glucosaminyl-(1->4)-N-acetyl-beta-D-glucosamine
originalData.definition: A β-D-glucosaminyl-(1→4)-N-acetyl-D-glucosamine in which the anomeric hydroxy group has β configuration.

originalData.title: beta-D-glucosaminyl-(1->4)-beta-D-glucosamine
originalData.definition: A β-<small>D</small>-glucosaminyl-(1→4)-<small>D</small>-glucosamine having β-configuration at the reducing end anomeric centre.

originalData.title: N-acetyl-beta-D-glucosaminyl-(1->4)-alpha-D-glucosamine
originalData.definition: The α-anomer of N-acetyl-β-D-glucosaminyl-(1→4)-D-glucosamine.

originalData.title: N-acetyl-beta-D-glucosaminyl-(1->4)-beta-D-glucosamine
originalData.definition: The β-anomer of N-acetyl-β-D-glucosaminyl-(1→4)-D-glucosamine.

originalData.title: 6-O-methyl-D-glucose
originalData.definition: A <small>D</small>-aldohexose that is <small>D</small>-glucose in which the hydrogen of the hydroxy group at position 6 has been substituted by a methyl group.

originalData.title: 3-O-methyl-D-glucose
originalData.definition: A <small>D</small>-aldohexose that is <small>D</small>-glucose in which the hydrogen of the hydroxy group at position 3 has been substituted by a methyl group. It is a non-metabolisable glucose analogue that is not phosphorylated by hexokinase and is used as a marker to assess glucose transport by evaluating its uptake within various cells and organ systems.

originalData.title: aldehydo-D-mannosamine
originalData.definition: A D-mannosamine in open-chain aldehydo-form.

originalData.title: 2-amino-2-deoxy-D-mannopyranose
originalData.definition: A <small>D</small>-mannosamine in cyclic pyranose form.

originalData.title: 3-[beta-D-galactosyl-(1->6)-beta-D-galactosyl]-1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol
originalData.definition: A digalactosylglycerol derivative in which the digalactosyl moiety is β-<small>D</small>-galactosyl-(1→6)-β-<small>D</small>-galactosyl at O-3, with O-1 and O-2 both acylated.

originalData.title: 2-amino-2-deoxy-D-mannonic acid
originalData.definition: A mannonic acid derivative in which the hydroxy group at position 2 has been replaced by an amino group.

originalData.title: L-gulonic acid 6-phosphate
originalData.definition: A gulonic acid derivative that is <small>L</small>-gulonic acid carrying a phosphate group at position 6.

originalData.title: 6-O-{5-hydroxy-4-oxo-3-[2-(sulfooxy)phenyl]-4H-chromen-6-yl}-L-gulonic acid
originalData.definition: A polycyclic compound comprising a 5-hydroxy-4H-chromen-4-one core with 3-sulfooxyphenyl and 6-gulonate substituents; which can act as a non-peptide antigen.

originalData.title: 6-O-[5-hydroxy-3-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-4-oxo-4H-chromen-6-yl]-L-gulonic acid
originalData.definition: The product of desulfonating 6-O-{5-hydroxy-4-oxo-3-[2-(sulfooxy)phenyl]-4H-chromen-6-yl}-L-gulonic acid.

originalData.title: 2-carboxy-D-arabinitol
originalData.definition: The 2-carboxy derivative of <small>D</small>-arabinitol.

originalData.title: 6-O-[alpha-D-mannosyl-(1->4)-alpha-D-glucosaminyl]-O-acyl-1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol
originalData.definition: A 6-O-[α-D-mannosyl-(1→4)-α-D-glucosaminyl]-1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol compound having an undefined acyl substituent in an unspecified position on the inositol ring.

originalData.title: PDPT 14:0/18:1
originalData.definition: A 1,2-diacyl-phosphatidyldimethylpropanethiol in which the two acyl substituents at positions 1 and 2 contain 14 carbons and no double bonds (tetradecanoyl) and 18 carbons and 1 double bond (octadecenoyl) respectively.

originalData.title: 1-palmitoylglycerol 3-phosphate
originalData.definition: A monoacylglycerol phosphate having palmitoyl as the acyl group on O-1 and with the phosphate group on O-3.

originalData.title: (S,S)-bis(2-oleoylglycero)-1-phosphate
originalData.definition: A 1,1'-lysobisphosphatidic acid in which both acyl groups are specified as oleoyl.

originalData.title: (S,S)-bis-(3-oleoylglycero)-1-phosphate
originalData.definition: A 2,2'-lysobisphosphatidic acid in which both acyl groups are specified as oleoyl.

originalData.title: 1-alpha-linolenoyl-2-hexadecanoylphosphatidylglycerol
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylglycerol (18:3/16:0) in which the 1- and 2-acyl groups are specified as α-linolenoyl and hexadecanoyl respectoively.

originalData.title: 1,2-dihexadecanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-(1'-sn-glycerol-3'-phosphate)
originalData.definition: A 3-(3-sn-phosphatidyl)-sn-glycerol 1-phosphate in which both phosphatidyl acyl groups are specified as hexadecanoyl (palmitoyl).

originalData.title: 1-oleoyl-2-hexadecanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-(1'-sn-glycerol-3'-phosphate)
originalData.definition: A 3-(3-sn-phosphatidyl)-sn-glycerol 1-phosphate in which the phosphatidyl acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specified as oleoyl and hexadecanoyl respectively.

originalData.title: 1,2-dioleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-(1'-sn-glycerol-3'-phosphate)
originalData.definition: A 3-(3-sn-phosphatidyl)-sn-glycerol 1-phosphate in which both phosphatidyl acyl groups are specified as oleoyl.

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol (18:2/18:2)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 36:4 in which both acyl groups contain 18 carbons and 2 double bonds.

originalData.title: PI(16:0_20:4)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 36:4 in which one acyl group has 16 carbons and is fully saturated while the other has 20 carbons and 4 double bonds (position not specified).

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol(16:0/20:4)
originalData.definition: A 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol in which the 1- and 2-acyl groups are hexadecanoyl and icosatetraenoyl respectively.

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol(18:0/18:2)
originalData.definition: A 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol in which the 1- and 2-acyl groups are octadecanoyl and octadecadienoyl respectively.

originalData.title: PI(18:0_20:4)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 38:4 in which one acyl group has 18 carbons and is fully saturated while the other has 20 carbons and 4 double bonds (position not specified).

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol (16:0/18:1)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 34:1 in which the acyl group at position 1 contains 16 carbons and no double bonds while that at position 2 contains 18 carbons and 1 double bond.

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol (16:1/18:0)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 34:1 in which the acyl group at position 1 contains 16 carbons and 1 double bond while that at position 2 contains 18 carbons and no double bonds.

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol (16:1/18:1)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 34:2 in which the acyl group at position 1 contains 16 carbons and 1 double bond while that at position 2 contains 18 carbons and 1 double bond.

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol (16:0/18:2)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 34:2 in which the acyl group at C-1 contains 16 carbons and no double bonds while that at C-2 contains 18 carbons and 2 double bonds

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol (18:0/18:1)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 36:1 in which the acyl group at position 1 contains 18 carbons and no double bonds while that at position 2 contains 18 carbons and 1 double bond.

originalData.title: PI(18:0_18:1)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 36:1 in which one acyl group has 18 carbons and is fully saturated while the other has 18 carbons and 1 double bond (position not specified).

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol (16:0/18:3)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 34:3 in which the acyl group at C-1 contains 16 carbons and no double bonds while that at C-2 contains 18 carbons and 3 double bonds

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol (18:0/18:3)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 36:3 in which the acyl group at C-1 contains 18 carbons and no double bonds while that at C-2 contains 18 carbons and 3 double bonds

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol (18:1/18:2)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 36:3 in which the acyl group at C-1 contains 18 carbons and 1 double bond while that at C-2 contains 18 carbons and 2 double bonds

originalData.title: PI(16:0_16:1)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 32:1 in which one acyl group has 16 carbons and is fully saturated while the other has 16 carbons and 1 double bonds (position not specified).

originalData.title: PI(18:0_20:3)
originalData.definition: A phosphatidylinositol 38:3 in which one acyl group has 18 carbons and is fully saturated while the other has 20 carbons and 3 double bonds (position not specified).

originalData.title: 1-stearoyl-2-dihomo-linolenoyl-GPI (18:0/20:3n3 or n6)
originalData.definition: A 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol in which the acyl group at position 1 is specified as octadecanoyl and the acyl group at position 2 is specified as either (11Z,14Z,17Z)-icosatrienoyl (dihomo-alpha-linolenoyl) or (8Z,11Z,14Z)-icosatrienoyl (dihomo-gamma-linolenoyl) respectively.

originalData.title: 1-hexadecanoyl-2-(5Z,8Z,11Z,14Z-icosatetraenoyl)-sn-glycero-3-phospho-D-myo-inositol
originalData.definition: A 1-hexadecanoyl-2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol in which the 2-acyl group is specified as (5Z,8Z,11Z,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl (arachidonyl).

originalData.title: 1-hexadecanoyl-2-(9Z,12Z-octadecadienoyl)-sn-glycero-3-phospho-D-myo-inositol
originalData.definition: A 1-hexadecanoyl-2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol in which the 2-acyl group is specified as 9Z,12Z-octadecadienoyl (linoleoyl).

originalData.title: 1-hexadecanoyl-2-(9Z-octadecenoyl)-sn-glycero-3-phospho-D-myo-inositol
originalData.definition: A 1-hexadecanoyl-2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol in which the 2-acyl group is specified as 9Z-octadecenoyl (oleoyl).

originalData.title: 1-hexadecanoyl-2-octadecanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-D-myo-inositol
originalData.definition: A 1-hexadecanoyl-2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol in which the 2-acyl group is specified as octadecanoyl (stearoyl).

originalData.title: 1,2-dihexadecanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-D-myo-inositol
originalData.definition: A 1-hexadecanoyl-2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol in which the 2-acyl group is also hexadecanoyl.

originalData.title: 1-stearoyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol
originalData.definition: A 1-octadecanoyl-2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol in which the 2-acyl group is specified as linoleoyl.

originalData.title: 1-stearoyl-2-oleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol
originalData.definition: A 1-octadecanoyl-2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol in which the 2-acyl group is specified as oleoyl.

originalData.title: 1-(10Z)-heptadecenoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol
originalData.definition: 1-acyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-D-myo-inositol in which the 1-acyl group is specified as (10Z)-heptadecenoyl.

originalData.title: 1-oleoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol
originalData.definition: 1-acyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-D-myo-inositol in which the 1-acyl group is specified as oleoyl.

originalData.title: phosphatidylinositol trisphosphate
originalData.definition: A derivative of phosphatidylinositol in which the inositol ring is phosphorylated at three unspecified positions.

originalData.title: DGDG(18:1(9Z)/18:3)
originalData.definition: A digalactosyldiacylglycerol in which the acyl group at position 1 is specified as (9Z)-octadecenoyl and the acyl group at position 2 is specified as an octadecatrienoyl group.

originalData.title: 6-oxoprostaglandin F1alpha
originalData.definition: A prostaglandin F<small><sub>α</sub></small> that is prostaglandin F<small><sub>1α</sub></small> bearing a keto substituent at the 6-position.

originalData.title: 15-oxoprostaglandin F2alpha
originalData.definition: A prostaglandin F<small><sub>α</sub></small> obtained by formal oxidation of the 15-hydroxy group of prostaglandin F<small><sub>2α</sub></small>.

originalData.title: prostaglandin F3alpha
originalData.definition: A prostaglandin F<small><sub>α</sub></small> that is prosta-5,13,17-trien-1-oic acid carrying three hydroxy substituents at positions 9α, 11α and 15.

originalData.title: 15-ketoprostaglandin F1alpha
originalData.definition: A prostaglandin F<small><sub>α</sub></small> that is prostaglandin F<small><sub>1α</sub></small> bearing a keto substituent at the 15-position. It is the initial metabolite of prostaglandin F<small><sub>1α</sub></small> via 15-hydroxyprostaglandin dehydrogenase.

originalData.title: 6,15-diketo,13,14-dihydroprostaglandin F1alpha
originalData.definition: A prostaglandin F<small><sub>α</sub></small> that is prostaglandin F<small><sub>1α</sub></small> bearing keto substituents at positions 6 and 15.

originalData.title: 17-phenyl-trinor-prostaglandin F2alpha
originalData.definition: A member of the class of prostaglandins F<small><sub>α</sub></small> that is latanoprost free acid with a double bond at position 13.

originalData.title: prostaglandin F2beta
originalData.definition: A prostaglandins F<small><sub>α</sub></small> that is prosta-5,13-dien-1-oic acid substituted by hydroxy groups at positions 9, 11 and 15. It is the 9β-hydroxy epimer of prostaglandin F<small><sub>2α</sub></small>.

originalData.title: crotonoyl-CoA
originalData.definition: The (E)-isomer of but-2-enoyl-CoA.

originalData.title: L-3-aminobutanoyl-CoA
originalData.definition: A 3-aminobutyryl-CoA having that results from the formal condensation of the thiol group of coenzyme A with the carboxy group of <small>L</small>-3-aminobutanoic acid.

originalData.title: (+)-dihydrocarvone
originalData.definition: A dihydrocarvone in (R,R) configuration.

originalData.title: (-)-isodihydrocarvone
originalData.definition: The (1R,4S)-stereoisomer of dihydrocarvone.

originalData.title: (+)-isodihydrocarvone
originalData.definition: The (1S,4R)-stereoisomer of dihydrocarvone.

originalData.title: (-)-dihydrocarvone
originalData.definition: A dihydrocarvone in (S,S) configuration.

originalData.title: lactucaxanthin
originalData.definition: A tunaxanthin that consists of ε,ε-carotene bearing hydroxy substituents at positions 3 and 3' (the 3S,3'S,6S,6'S-diastereomer).

originalData.title: D-mannopyranuronic acid
originalData.definition: The pyranose form of <small>D</small>-mannonic acid.

originalData.title: keto-D-gluconic acid
originalData.definition: A ketogluconic acid of <small>D</small>-configuration.

originalData.title: 2-dehydro-L-gluconic acid
originalData.definition: A  ketogluconic acid that is <small>L</small>-gluconic acid in which the hydroxy group at position 2 has been oxidized to a keto group.

originalData.title: D-galacturonic acid
originalData.definition: The <small>D</small>-enantiomer of galacturonic acid. It is the main component of pectin.

originalData.title: 4-(4-deoxy-beta-D-gluc-4-enosyluronic acid)-D-galacturonic acid
originalData.definition: A D-galacturonic acid having a 4-deoxy-α-L-threo-hex-4-enopyranosyluronic acid moiety attached at the 4-position.

originalData.title: D-glucuronic acid
originalData.definition: The <small>D</small>-enantiomer of glucuronic acid.

originalData.title: L-glucuronic acid
originalData.definition: The <small>L</small>-enantiomer of glucuronic acid.

originalData.title: idopyranuronic acid
originalData.definition: The pyranose form of iduronic acid.

originalData.title: L-iduronic acid
originalData.definition: Any iduronic acid having <small>L</small>-configuration.

originalData.title: (2R,3Z)-phycocyanobilin
originalData.definition: The (3Z)-isomer of (2R)-phycocyanobilin.

originalData.title: 1-oleoylglycerone 3-phosphate
originalData.definition: A 1-acylglycerone 3-phosphate in which the acyl group is specified as oleoyl.

originalData.title: 1-linoleoylglycerone 3-phosphate
originalData.definition: A 1-acylglycerone 3-phosphate in which the acyl group is specified as linoleoyl.

originalData.title: 1-arachidonoylglycerone 3-phosphate
originalData.definition: A 1-acylglycerone 3-phosphate in which the acyl group is specified as arachidonoyl.

originalData.title: ubiquinone-0
originalData.definition: A derivative of benzoquinone carrying a 5-methyl substituent; and methoxy substituents at positions 2 and 3. The core structure of the ubiquinone group of compounds.

originalData.title: ubiquinone-1
originalData.definition: A compound composed of the standard 2,3-dimethoxy-5-methylbenzoquinone nucleus common to ubiquinones; and a side chain of a single isoprenoid unit.

originalData.title: coenzyme Q10
originalData.definition: A ubiquinone having a side chain of 10 isoprenoid units. In the naturally occurring isomer, all isoprenyl double bonds are in the E- configuration.

originalData.title: ubiquinone-5
originalData.definition: A compound composed of the 2,3-dimethoxy-5-methylbenzoquinone nucleus common to ubiquinones; and a side chain of five isoprenoid units.

originalData.title: ubiquinone-2
originalData.definition: A compound composed of the 2,3-dimethoxy-5-methylbenzoquinone nucleus common to ubiquinones; and a side chain of two isoprenoid units.

originalData.title: ubiquinone-7
originalData.definition: A compound whose structure comprises a 2,3-dimethoxy-5-methylbenzoquinone nucleus, common to ubiquinones; and a side chain of seven isoprenoid units.

originalData.title: ubiquinone-6
originalData.definition: A ubiquinone compound having a (2E,6E,10E,14E,18E)-3,7,11,15,19,23-hexamethyltetracosa-2,6,10,14,18,22-hexaen-1-yl substituent at position 2.

originalData.title: ubiquinone-8
originalData.definition: A ubiquinone whose structure comprises a 2,3-dimethoxy-5-methylbenzoquinone nucleus (common to all ubiquinones) and a side chain of eight isoprenoid units.

originalData.title: plastoquinone
originalData.definition: A polyprenylbenzoquinone that is 2,3-dimethylbenzoquinone with a polyprenyl side chain at position 5. There are several naturally-occurring plastoquinones with side chains of different length (containing between 6 and 9 isoprene units).

originalData.title: 2-methoxy-6-all-trans-polyprenyl-1,4-benzoquinone
originalData.definition: An all-trans-polyprenylquinone in which the methoxy group is at position C-2 and polyprenyl group is at position C-6.

originalData.title: 2-methoxy-5-methyl-6-all-trans-polyprenylbenzoquinone
originalData.definition: An all-trans-polyprenylbenzoquinone in which the polyprenyl substituent is at C-2 together with additional methyl and methoxy groups present at C3 and C-6 respectively.

originalData.title: menaquinone
originalData.definition: Any member of the class of menaquinones that is 2-methyl-1,4-naphthoquinone with an unsaturated isoprenoid chain at the 3-position.

originalData.title: phylloquinone
originalData.definition: A member of the class of phylloquinones that consists of 1,4-naphthoquinone having methyl and phytyl groups at positions 2 and 3 respectively. The parent of the class of phylloquinones.

originalData.title: 2-phytyl-1,4-naphthoquinone
originalData.definition: A phylloquinone that consists of 1,4-naphthoquinone bearing a phytyl group at position 2.

originalData.title: 8-methylmenaquinone-6
originalData.definition: A menaquinone whose side-chain contains six isoprene units in an all-trans-configuration and in which the hydrogen at position 8 on the naphthoquinone ring has been replaced by a methyl group..

originalData.title: beta-dihydromenaquinone-9
originalData.definition: A member of the class of menaquinones that is menaquinone-9 in which the double bond that is second nearest to the naphthoquinone moiety has been dihydrogenated.

originalData.title: 8-methylmenaquinone
originalData.definition: Any member of the class of 8-methylmenaquinones that is 2,8-dimethyl-1,4-naphthoquinone with an unsaturated isoprenoid chain at the 3-position.

originalData.title: 3-hexenal
originalData.definition: A hexenal carrying an unsaturation at position 3.

originalData.title: (3E)-2,3,4-trimethylhex-3-enal
originalData.definition: A hexenal that is (3E)-hex-3-enal substituted by methyl groups at positions 2, 3 and 6. Metabolite observed in cancer metabolism.

originalData.title: 2,11-tetradecadienal
